title: erysimum witmannii subsp. transsilvanicum
intro: Erysimum witmannii subsp. transsilvanicum, also known as Transylvanian wallflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its vibrant yellow, orange, or red flowers. Traditionally, the plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as colds, flu, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Erysimum witmannii subsp. transsilvanicum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve respiratory health. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used in the form of capsules or dried flowers.
